memoryArtifacts.description: If you are related to John Howland and Elizabeth Tilley then you are also related to three other Mayflower Pilgrims, John and Mrs. John Tilley and their daughter, Elizabeth Tilley.

zh.lifeSummary: 當James Brown於1623年出生在英國時，他的父親John Abram Brown47歲，母親Dorothy Unknown39歲。他於1655年與Lydia Howland在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1710年10月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托斯萬希亞享年87歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托斯萬希亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Brown于1623年出生在英国时，他的父亲John Abram Brown47岁，母亲Dorothy Unknown39岁。他于1655年与Lydia Howland在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1710年10月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托斯萬希亞享年87岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托斯萬希亞。

nameMeanings.name: Brown
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Brunson
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Brun (Old English Brūna or Old Norse Brúni) + -son ‘Brown's son’. See Brown . There may also be occasional survivals of an Old English compound personal name Brūnsunu ‘brown son’, attested in the Domesday Book, and presumably given to a child with brown hair or complexion. Whether it was also used as an adult nickname is uncertain. This form of the surname is most common in Norfolk.

variants.name: Browning
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Brouning (Old English Brūning), a derivative of the byname Brūn (see Brown ).

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. William Browning was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Browne
meanings: English: variant of   Brown  .
Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Burnett
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Tyrone and Antrim): descriptive nickname of Norman origin, from Old French burnete, a diminutive of brun ‘brown’ (see Brown ), or possibly in some instances a metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of burnete, a high quality woolen cloth, originally dark brown in color.
Probably also an altered form of English   Barnett  .
In some cases also an altered form of French   Burnet  ; compare   Burnette  .

variants.name: Erjavec
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from an old spelling of rjavec, most commonly meaning ‘dark skinned man’ and ‘brown horse’. Compare Brown .

variants.name: Borron
meanings: Scottish: perhaps a variant of   Brown   in a Scottish pronunciation. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Broun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Lyubov, Sergey.
Scottish: variant of   Brown  .
Jewish: variant of   Braun  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When James Brown was born in 1623, in England, his father, John Abram Brown, was 47 and his mother, Dorothy Unknown, was 39. He married Lydia Howland in 1655, in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 29 October 1710, in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 87, and was buried in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Brown est né en 1623, à Angleterre, son père, John Abram Brown, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Unknown, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Lydia Howland en 1655, à Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 29 octobre 1710, à Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterré à Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Brown 1623 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Abram Brown 47 und seine Mutter Dorothy Unknown 39. Er hat 1655 in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Lydia Howland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 29. Oktober 1710 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Brown è nato il nel 1623 nel England, il suo padre, John Abram Brown, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Dorothy Unknown, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Lydia Howland nel 1655, nel Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 29 ottobre 1710, nel Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 87 anni, e fu sepolto a Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: James Brownが1623年にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Abram Brownは47歳，母Dorothy Unknownは39歳であった。1655年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーでLydia Howlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1710年10月29日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルスワンセアで87歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルスワンセアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Brown(이)가 1623년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Abram Brown(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy Unknown(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1655년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이에서 Lydia Howland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1710년 10월 29일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 스완시에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 스완시에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Brown nasceu em 1623, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Abram Brown, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Unknown, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Lydia Howland em 1655, em Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de outubro de 1710, em Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 87 anos, e foi sepultado em Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Brown родился в 1623 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Abram Brown было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Unknown, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Lydia Howland в 1655 г. в местности массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 29 октября 1710 г. в местности свансеа, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 87 лет и был похоронен в местности свансеа, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

Se casó con Lydia Howland en 1655, en Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 29 de octubre de 1710, en Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultado en Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
